company_type: Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) - Multifamily Properties

supply_chain.dependencies: Reliance on contractors and subcontractors for development and construction activities; dependence on materials and labor supply for construction projects
supply_chain.risks: Disruptions in the supply of materials or labor; increased materials and labor costs; problems with contractors or subcontractors; shortages of materials; errors and omissions in design or construction process

regulatory_risks: Rent control or rent stabilization laws and regulations in certain states and local municipalities
Restrictions on amounts of rent increases
Limitations on ability to enforce contractual rental obligations
Restrictions on ability to charge certain fees
Inability to recover increases in operating expenses
Difficulty in disposing of properties in certain circumstances due to rent control laws
Zoning, land-use, building, occupancy permit requirements
REIT qualification requirements under Internal Revenue Code
Requirement to distribute annual dividends to shareholders equal to minimum of 90% of adjusted taxable income
Federal and state income tax risks if REIT qualification fails
State and local income, franchise, and excise taxes
Changes in tax laws and administrative interpretations
